findings.title: Practice profiles encode firm-specific governance and decision policy

evidence.description: The profile records approved and prohibited use cases, conditions, red lines, risk tiers, and approval paths.
The profile records vendor standards, acceptable fallbacks, never positions, and an automatic-no term.
The profile records issue-specific handling and escalation routes.

findings.proves: The static design intentionally represents organization-specific choices, prohibitions, fallbacks, and escalation logic as reusable operating policy.
findings.does_not_prove: It does not establish provider retention, cross-customer pooling, generalized training, or economic value of the resulting profile.
findings.alternative_explanation: Customer-specific configuration is necessary to deliver requested, correctly calibrated legal assistance.
findings.test_to_confirm: Observe a deployed session and trace profile reads, model requests, logs, retention, and tenant boundaries under authenticated controls.

findings.title: User-specific policy is durably stored outside the replaceable plugin cache

evidence.description: Configuration is written to a version-independent local path, copied forward from old caches, never written into the template, and a company profile is shared across plugins.
The interview writes the regulatory watchlist and materiality threshold to the local configuration and distinguishes configured from tested connectors.

findings.proves: The design creates durable, reusable customer-side configuration and makes it authoritative for subsequent work.
findings.does_not_prove: A local path is not evidence that data stays off provider inference systems, logs, backups, or support systems while used.
findings.alternative_explanation: Version-independent local storage protects user customization from plugin upgrades and improves portability.
findings.test_to_confirm: Instrument filesystem, network, and hosted-session behavior while reading and using the profile; obtain the applicable retention and data-use terms.

findings.title: Managed agents can read legal repositories through remote MCP services and request outbound handoffs

evidence.description: A read-only document worker is limited to configured VDR MCP services but has Box and Google Drive toolsets enabled and remote MCP URLs.
A headless watcher produces a report and requests a Slack send handoff while treating contract text as untrusted.
The orchestrator is local-tool scoped, remote legal/document MCP servers are declared, and only a designated leaf has Write.

findings.proves: The static managed-agent architecture includes remote document-access boundaries and a proposed external notification path with explicit separation of read and write authority.
findings.does_not_prove: It does not show actual deployment, credentials, payloads, Slack delivery, provider retention, or secondary use.
findings.alternative_explanation: These are ordinary customer-authorized connectors needed to perform diligence and deadline monitoring, with deliberate least-authority controls.
findings.test_to_confirm: Deploy in a test tenant, capture MCP requests and handoff approvals, verify scopes and destinations, and inspect logs and retention.

findings.title: Regulatory calibration is designed as a continuing customer-directed learning loop

evidence.description: The interview elicits example-specific immediate, digest, or ignore judgments to build a materiality threshold.
The profile is described as improving through output feedback, monitoring, and user-directed playbook or threshold edits.

findings.proves: The repository implements a feedback process that converts user judgments about signal into durable customer-specific monitoring policy.
findings.does_not_prove: It does not show automatic behavioral learning, hidden feedback collection, model-weight training, or transfer between customers.
findings.alternative_explanation: Explicit editable configuration is a transparent, customer-controlled alternative to opaque learning.
findings.test_to_confirm: Verify whether deployed edits occur only after explicit user action and whether feedback or before/after profiles enter provider analytics.

findings.title: Community skill installation creates a governed but non-zero supply-chain trust boundary

evidence.description: The installer fetches third-party content, displays raw instructions, checks execution surfaces, requires fresh approval, then writes the skill and an audit log.
Restrictive mode requires fetch and analysis in a read-only subagent and fails closed when that isolation is unavailable.
Updates are re-scanned, security-surface diffs force review, and the documentation admits defenses cannot eliminate malicious-skill risk.

findings.proves: The design intentionally imports executable instruction packages from third parties while applying deterministic and human trust gates.
findings.does_not_prove: It does not identify a malicious package, a bypass, an executed payload, or an Anthropic-operated secondary-use path.
findings.alternative_explanation: The unusually explicit isolation, allowlist, diff, and approval controls substantially reduce ordinary community-package risk.
findings.test_to_confirm: Exercise adversarial fixtures against restrictive and permissive modes and verify enforcement in the actual installer runtime.

findings.title: Matter histories retain sensitive decision context but cross-matter reads are default-denied

evidence.description: New matters seed matter, history, and notes files; closure archives rather than deletes; cross-matter reads require the flag.
Matter folders store client/counterparty facts, dated decisions, drafts, reviews, and working notes.
Cross-matter context is off by default, explicit user request remains required when on, and retention enforcement is out of scope.

findings.proves: The design retains a rich local matter decision history while specifying a strong prompt-level default against reading other matters.
findings.does_not_prove: Prompt instructions alone do not establish runtime sandbox enforcement, secure deletion, tenant isolation, or absence from model-service logs.
findings.alternative_explanation: Matter separation and explicit cross-matter opt-in are affirmative confidentiality controls; archival retention supports conflicts and recordkeeping.
findings.test_to_confirm: Attempt cross-matter reads in the deployed product, inspect filesystem/tool enforcement, and test deletion and retention behavior.

new_patterns.name: durable-policy-profile
new_patterns.logic: Find version-independent configuration that records customer standards, fallbacks, red lines, approvals, or routing and is reread across workflows.
new_patterns.false_positive_risk: Templates may contain only placeholders and may never be populated or used in a deployed tenant.

new_patterns.name: explicit-calibration-loop
new_patterns.logic: Connect elicited example judgments to a persisted rubric, repeated filtering, and user-directed policy updates.
new_patterns.false_positive_risk: Marketing language about learning may describe manual configuration rather than automated learning.

new_patterns.name: remote-read-local-write-handoff
new_patterns.logic: Trace remote MCP reads through constrained workers to local artifacts and separately gated outbound or write-capable leaves.
new_patterns.false_positive_risk: Declared connectors and handoffs may be unconfigured examples rather than active runtime paths.

new_patterns.name: instruction-package-supply-chain
new_patterns.logic: Trace third-party instruction fetch, trust scanning, approval, persistent installation, update diffing, and later execution.
new_patterns.false_positive_risk: Strong fail-closed controls may prevent the theoretical package path from becoming exploitable.

negative_evidence.description: Cross-matter context is off by default and even when enabled requires an explicit cross-matter request.
negative_evidence.effect: Weighs against ambient cross-matter aggregation and supports H0.

negative_evidence.description: The VDR reader is read-only, treats documents as untrusted, caps output, validates a schema, and limits outbound access to declared MCP services.
The orchestrator defaults tools off, enables only local read/search tools, isolates remote connectors in leaves, and uses a human handoff request.
negative_evidence.effect: Shows deliberate least-authority and human-review architecture.

negative_evidence.description: Restrictive mode fails closed without an isolated read-only scanner, requires raw-source display and a fresh typed yes, and rescans security-sensitive updates.
negative_evidence.effect: Strong counterevidence to an interpretation that third-party instructions are silently installed.

negative_evidence.description: Setup says it does not read personal Claude history, other conversations, or a home-directory CLAUDE.md, and asks before using current-conversation context.
negative_evidence.effect: Weighs against undisclosed ambient history capture in this static workflow.

negative_evidence.description: High-consequence notices remain drafts, require explicit engagement with a lawyer-facing gate, and are not auto-submitted.
negative_evidence.effect: Supports a human-accountability design rather than autonomous legal action.

unknowns.question: What profile, matter, prompt, completion, tool-call, and feedback fields reach Anthropic-hosted services in deployed Cowork or Managed Agents?
unknowns.why_source_cannot_answer: The repository describes prompts and manifests, not proprietary request construction, telemetry, or hosted runtime behavior.
unknowns.needed_evidence: Authenticated network captures, tenant logs, product documentation, and provider attestations tied to the tested deployment.

unknowns.question: How long are profile-derived signals, MCP payloads, reports, and support artifacts retained and for what secondary purposes?
unknowns.why_source_cannot_answer: No provider retention or support-system implementation is present in the pinned source.
unknowns.needed_evidence: Applicable contract, DPA, retention schedule, support policy, and deletion-test receipts.

unknowns.question: Are prompt-level cross-matter prohibitions technically enforced?
unknowns.why_source_cannot_answer: The visible control is an instruction and local flag; the tool sandbox and hosted runtime are closed.
unknowns.needed_evidence: Adversarial runtime tests, access-control configuration, and audit logs for denied reads.

unknowns.question: Are customer calibration edits or accepted/rejected outputs used for generalized product or model improvement?
unknowns.why_source_cannot_answer: The source shows explicit customer-side edits but no feedback ingestion, training pipeline, or cross-tenant aggregation.
unknowns.needed_evidence: Feature telemetry schema, feedback pipeline documentation, training-data governance, and tenant-specific opt-in records.

unknowns.question: Which declared MCP connectors are enabled and what scopes, operators, logs, and retention apply?
unknowns.why_source_cannot_answer: Static manifests list endpoints or environment variables but do not establish authentication, deployment, or operator-side behavior.
unknowns.needed_evidence: Tenant connector inventory, OAuth scopes, MCP server ownership, request logs, and retention terms.

unknowns.question: Do community-skill trust gates behave as specified under adversarial content and updates?
unknowns.why_source_cannot_answer: The assigned source is a natural-language skill contract, not an executed enforcement trace.
unknowns.needed_evidence: Runtime harness results for prompt injection, Unicode obfuscation, permission expansion, update diffs, and fail-closed isolation.
